title: WITNESS: add bridge witness-realization vocabulary over signer sessions
description:
Context:
- Tusk parent: tusk-g7p.
- Fish has the upstream paid-challenge witness-realization meaning note, and Kurma now carries a Premath-bound witness realization method.
- Bridge owns the downstream domain contract over signer sessions, bounded materialization, and audit/session artifacts.

Goal:
Add a bridge-owned typed witness-realization vocabulary over the signer-session and materialization artifacts bridge already owns.

Scope:
- define the bridge-local vocabulary for Challenge, StateRead, ActionSelection, WitnessPlan, WitnessArtifact, ValidatorProfile, ValidationResult, and StateTransition as it applies to bridge signing and session artifacts
- layer that vocabulary over MaterializationSession, SignatureRequest, SignatureResponse, audit records/envelopes, and compatibility witnesses without replacing those existing types
- add a schema and example fixture if the vocabulary needs a concrete machine-checkable carrier
- wire the new surface into the SECRET-0002 signer/session docs, the type-family index, and any checksum/conformance manifests touched by the new files

Non-goals:
- no provider-specific Tempo, OpenAI, payment, or funded-runtime quirks
- no Premath kernel doctrine in bridge; cite upstream meaning only as a boundary
- no downstream secret file paths, local deployment proof, or wallet/provider runtime implementation
- no widening bridge beyond typed domain contract ownership

Verification:
- scripts/bridge-conformance-check.sh --repo .
- scripts/bridge-property-check.sh --repo . if planner or handoff semantics are touched
- rg "WitnessRealization|witness realization|ValidationResult|StateTransition" specs/secrets/secret-0002 specs/TYPE-FAMILIES.md

Landing:
- one visible git commit in a dedicated bridge worktree, then close both this bridge issue and the Tusk parent once the bridge endpoint is landed.

Required Skills:
bridge
close_reason: completed on main in commit eb3e15b after bridge-conformance-check, bridge-property-check, git diff --check, and targeted witness-realization schema validation passed

title: INTERPRETATION: make bridge carriers normative and tighten overlap plus audit identity
description:
Goal:
Promote the bridge Interpretation family from note-only prose into normative bridge-owned carriers and tighten the identity surfaces that currently drift across Premath context, admission assembly, and audit.

Scope:
- add the bridge-owned carrier surface needed to make Interpretation concrete, including source-context and interpretation-binding artifacts and a typed interpreted-observation or equivalent normative carrier
- wire the interpretation surface into specs/bridge-adapter/adapter-contract.md, specs/bridge-adapter/provider-mapping.yaml, and the relevant schemas and examples so the family is carried rather than merely described
- tighten BRIDGE-PREMATH-0001 request overlap identity so delegated actor and consumer kind do not collapse distinct authority-bearing requests
- update the reference adapter and audit example so emitted tool, resource, and domain identity come from the canonical admitted runtime request rather than raw witness-side values
- extend bridge conformance and property checks with interpretation fixtures for unknown, ambiguous, and stale interpretation states that must not reach allow or materialization

Non-goals:
- no Premath kernel JWT doctrine work in this issue
- no Kurma carriage or runtime storage and cache implementation
- no downstream provider runtime or funded proof wiring
- no SECRET-0002 realization cleanup; that is a separate wire

Verification:
- ./scripts/bridge-conformance-check.sh
- ./scripts/bridge-property-check.sh
- rg "act_for|consumer_kind|interpretation" specs/bridge-premath-0001 specs/bridge-adapter

Landing:
- one visible git commit in a dedicated .git-worktrees/<issue-id>-interpretation-carriers worktree
- if the existing Premath SourceProfile seam proves insufficient, file a separate Premath follow-up instead of widening this lane
design:
Context class: governed-runtime
Owning repo: bridge
Simplex: none for this lane; optional later 1-simplex follow-up with Premath only if the generic source-profile seam is insufficient
Wire:
- input context: current bridge adapter plus note-only Interpretation promotion from bridge-9x0
- output artifact: normative interpretation carriers plus tightened overlap and audit identity surfaces
- verification boundary: bridge conformance and property checks with interpretation fixtures
- landing boundary: one visible git commit in an issue-scoped worktree
acceptance_criteria:
- bridge has a normative interpretation carrier surface rather than only the note in interpretation-binding.md
- adapter, schema, and audit surfaces carry interpretation and source-context identity explicitly
- RequestOverlap no longer collapses distinct act_for or consumer_kind cases
- the reference adapter and audit example emit canonical admitted identifiers rather than raw witness-side identity
- bridge conformance and property checks cover unknown, ambiguous, and stale interpretation outcomes and keep them out of allow and materialization
close_reason: completed in visible commit ef23997 on branch bridge-fdy-interpretation-carriers after bridge-conformance-check and bridge-property-check passed

title: REALIZATION: remove SessionOpenArtifact and re-center signer flow on MaterializationSession
description:
Goal:
Remove the bridge-owned SessionOpenArtifact surface and re-center the signer-session contract on MaterializationSession plus SignatureRequest/SignatureResponse.

Scope:
- remove SessionOpenArtifact from specs/TYPE-FAMILIES.md, .agents/skills/bridge/references/BRIDGE-FLOW.md, scripts/bridge-conformance-check.sh, specs/secrets/secret-0002/SECRET-0002.backend-and-materialization-profile.md, and specs/secrets/secret-0002/SIGNER-SESSION-CONTRACT.md
- delete the SessionOpenArtifact schema and example fixture under specs/secrets/secret-0002/
- refresh specs/secrets/secret-0002/SHA256SUMS.txt
- keep MaterializationSession as the canonical session-open artifact and keep SignatureRequest/SignatureResponse as the narrow post-session protocol

Non-goals:
- no interpretation or admission-carrier work in this issue
- no downstream Tempo or home runtime modeling
- no bridge adapter or Premath profile changes

Verification:
- ./scripts/bridge-conformance-check.sh
- ./scripts/bridge-property-check.sh

Landing:
- one visible git commit in a dedicated .git-worktrees/<issue-id>-session-open-cleanup worktree
- default bridge checkout stays out of the implementation lane because it currently carries mixed local dirt
design:
Context class: governed-runtime
Owning repo: bridge
Simplex: none
Wire:
- input context: current signer-session contract with SessionOpenArtifact
- output artifact: narrowed bridge-owned signer flow without SessionOpenArtifact
- verification boundary: bridge conformance and property checks
- landing boundary: one visible git commit in an issue-scoped worktree
acceptance_criteria:
- SessionOpenArtifact is no longer named in the active bridge-owned signer and realization surface
- the schema and example fixture are removed
- the signer-session contract remains centered on MaterializationSession plus SignatureRequest and SignatureResponse
- bridge conformance and property checks pass

title: bridge-a6b followup: type §5 admission using IDENT0 identity simplex (stacked on bridge-a6b branch)
description:
Goal:
Extend the bridge-a6b wdog-operator-workstation-age-local PROFILE.md
§5 admission section to type "implicit operator consent" against
IDENT0's identity simplex [issuer, subject, credential, scope, epoch]
instead of leaving the admission kernel abstract. This makes the v0
ephemeral-envvar materialization sanctionable against a concrete
typed identity presentation.

Stacked on top of the bridge-a6b-workstation-bootstrap-profile
branch (commit 8a9829b), which itself is not yet merged to bridge
main. Branches merge to main in any order (a6b first then this
followup, or this squashed with a6b at operator discretion).

Scope:
- specs/secrets/profiles/wdog-operator-workstation-age-local/PROFILE.md
  §5 MaterializationSession: keep the existing session-record schema,
  add a new §5.x subsection "Identity presentation under IDENT0"
  that names the simplex roles concretely:
    * issuer = operator workstation's age public key (IDENT0.IssuerP
      = age pubkey)
    * subject = named operator principal (IDENT0.SubjectP = the
      committing author identity in the consuming repo)
    * credential = sops-encrypted ciphertext unwrapped via age
      (IDENT0.CredentialP)
    * scope = BackendBinding's name + authorityClass pair
      (IDENT0.ScopeP)
    * epoch = binding's rotation policy (IDENT0.EpochP; rotate-per-
      apply in v0)
  - Map the IDENT0 witnesses (SignedBy, Names, Grants, FreshAt,
    NotRevoked) onto the committed-BackendBinding surface:
    * SignedBy holds when the ciphertext's age recipient list
      includes the issuer
    * Names holds when the committed author matches subject
    * Grants holds when the binding's authorityClass matches scope
    * FreshAt holds when rotation is current
    * NotRevoked holds when no burn event has been declared
  - Keep §5's existing "implicit operator consent" wording in place;
    this extension types that consent as a typed identity presentation
    admissible under IDENT0, not a weakening.
  - Point at BRIDGE-PREMATH-0001.premath-gluing-profile §2.2 as the
    conformance surface that connects this admission to the rest of
    bridge context coordinates.
- Refresh README.md (same profile dir) to note the IDENT0 backing in
  one sentence.
- No schema changes (MaterializationSession shape unchanged).
- No admission-pathway schema additions (IDENT0 is a typing layer on
  the existing implicit-consent model).

Non-goals:
- No changes to bridge-premath-0001 (just landed in bridge-2u7;
  cross-references only).
- No new profiles.
- No bridge runtime work.

Verification:
- scripts/bridge-conformance-check.sh --repo . passes.
- rg "IDENT0|identity simplex" specs/secrets/profiles/wdog-operator-
  workstation-age-local/ returns the new §5.x + README line.
- PROFILE.md §5 still has the implicit-operator-consent wording.

Landing:
- One visible git commit on topic branch bridge-?-a6b-ident0-
  admission stacked on bridge-a6b-workstation-bootstrap-profile.
- Merge path: (a) a6b merges first, then this follow-up fast-forwards;
  OR (b) operator squash-merges both together; operator's choice.

Dependencies:
- bridge-a6b branch (unmerged commit 8a9829b) — stacked on this
  branch, so rebasing needed if a6b is amended.
- bridge-2u7 (landed on its own branch, unmerged): provides the
  BRIDGE-PREMATH-0001 §2.2 cross-reference target.
- premath-4ey (landed on premath main): provides the IDENT0 module
  path citation.
close_reason: Merged to bridge main in 1557f72 after bridge-conformance-check and bridge-property-check passed

comments.author: workingdoge
comments.text: Landed at 3665f97 on branch bridge-utp-a6b-ident0-admission (stacked on bridge-a6b-workstation-bootstrap-profile at 8a9829b). 2 files, +73/-1: PROFILE.md §5.1 Identity presentation under IDENT0 + README.md pointer. bridge-conformance-check passes (86 JSON files, 5 manifests, 111 entries). Issue stays open — merge to bridge main is operator-owned, after or alongside bridge-a6b.

title: BRIDGE-PREMATH-0001: cite premath ASP0 + IDENT0 modules by path, name identity simplex as first concrete PremathContext example
description:
Goal:
Refresh BRIDGE-PREMATH-0001.premath-gluing-profile.md to cite the
concrete premath module paths now available under
fish/sites/premath/specs/raw/ (landed via premath-4ey). The current
gluing profile cites "Premath kernel doctrine" abstractly; this lane
adds path-specific pointers so downstream consumers can trace bridge
context coordinates back to the specific premath modules that ground
them.

Scope:
- specs/bridge-premath-0001/BRIDGE-PREMATH-0001.premath-gluing-profile.md
  - §2 Relationship to Premath: name the specific premath modules
    supplying (C, Cov, Def, f*, locality, descent):
      * ASP0 (specs/raw/asp/ASP0.md) as the substrate / category
      * IDENT0 (specs/raw/ident/IDENT0.md) as the first concrete profile
        over ASP0
  - Add a short subsection naming IDENT0's identity simplex
    [issuer, subject, credential, scope, epoch] as the first concrete
    example of a PremathContext's identity coordinates. Cross-reference
    §4.2.1 Request coordinates (subject_id, witness_jti, consumer_id)
    as the bridge-side carriers of that simplex.
  - Name the JWT backends (JWT0, JWT0-OBS, JWT0-SEARCH, JWT1-JWKS0) as
    candidate backend realization profiles for identity at the
    secret-management seam. Does NOT commit bridge to consuming them —
    just names them as the first concrete place this could land.
- Refresh specs/bridge-premath-0001/SHA256SUMS.txt to match.

Non-goals:
- No schema changes. Purely descriptive / citation refresh.
- No changes to other specs (bridge-adapter/, secret-0001-3, etc.).
- No bridge-a6b body changes (§5 admission extension is a separate
  lane, post-a6b-merge).
- No workingdoge or tusk changes.

Verification:
- scripts/bridge-conformance-check.sh --repo . passes.
- rg 'specs/raw/asp/ASP0|specs/raw/ident/IDENT0' specs/bridge-premath-0001/
  returns the new citations.
- No other specs/ changes show in git diff.

Landing:
- One visible git commit on topic branch bridge-?-premath-citations
  (.git-worktrees/ convention). Branch merge to bridge main is
  operator-owned.

Dependencies:
- premath-4ey (landed 2026-04-17; makes IDENT0/ASP0 paths stable).
- Not blocked by bridge-a6b; branches independently off bridge main.

Why now:
premath-4ey just landed the ASP + IDENT kernel in specs/raw/. The
static stack flow premath → bridge → workingdoge begins with bridge
citing the kernel by path. Doing this refresh unblocks:
(a) a future bridge-a6b admission extension that uses IDENT0's
identity simplex instead of implicit operator consent, and
(b) workingdoge 6mu IAM shaping that maps IDENT0 roles onto
Cloudflare Zero Trust org semantics.
close_reason: Merged to bridge main in 1557f72 after bridge-conformance-check and bridge-property-check passed

title: Adopt tusk devenv consumer pattern (tusk-skill + ops-skill + bridge-skill)
description: Replace plain pkgs.mkShell with tusk.lib.mkRepoShell and import tusk.devenvModules.{tusk-skill, ops-skill, bridge-skill} so the bridge skill authored at .agents/skills/bridge/ stages into .codex/skills/bridge/ on shell entry, matching the aac consumer pattern. Also requires: devenv + devenv-root inputs, tusk pin bump (bridge-skill was added in tusk commit 4278e3c, current bridge pin d9ee4be predates it), and a bridge-specific .envrc (current file is a stray copy of aac's).
close_reason: Completed: devenv consumer shell stages tusk-skill, ops-skill, bridge-skill into .codex/skills/

title: bridge: add wdog-operator-workstation-age-local SECRET-0002 profile (bootstrap-class ephemeral envvar materializer)
description:
Goal:
File a new SECRET-0002 profile instance that admits operator-workstation-
rooted bootstrap secrets (e.g. HCLOUD_TOKEN, Cloudflare mutation token)
that must be materialized as ephemeral environment variables for a
provisioning subprocess (tofu apply, nixos-anywhere) before the v0 primary
exists. The existing wdog-v0-age-local profile is host-rooted and does
not admit workstation-side bootstrap flows.

Scope (this lane only — bridge-side profile doc):
- specs/secrets/profiles/wdog-operator-workstation-age-local/PROFILE.md
  - BackendProfile: age-encrypted-files-on-operator-workstation
    (recipient = operator's workstation age identity, NOT host).
  - MaterializerProfile: ephemeral-envvar-handle
    (materialization artifact = process env var in a short-lived
    provisioning subprocess; teardown = process exit; no persistence
    past the subprocess lifetime).
  - BackendBinding shape: names the scope class (bootstrap-mutation,
    bootstrap-observational), owning authority class (spine §2.6),
    recipient list requirements, rotation expectation (burn after
    each apply; rotate on operator machine change).
  - Admission model: implicit operator consent at plan-request time
    (same v0 concession as wdog-v0-age-local §3); gains explicit
    MaterializationSession when a bridge runtime ships.
  - Non-goals: not a host-materializer (belongs to wdog-v0-age-local);
    not a general secret store; not CI-accessible (CI runner and operator
    workstation are different trust anchors per spine §3.1).
- specs/secrets/profiles/wdog-operator-workstation-age-local/README.md
  - One-paragraph summary + pointer to PROFILE.md + expected downstream
    consumer (workingdoge cloud/host/secrets/bootstrap/).
- specs/secrets/profiles/README.md
  - Add entry to the profile index with a one-line scope description.

Non-goals (deferred to follow-up simplex endpoints):
- Downstream workingdoge instantiation (.sops.yaml creation-rules,
  workstation recipient age key registration, per-secret
  BackendBindings for hcloud.yaml + cloudflare-mutation.yaml): a
  separate workingdoge-?? issue, blocked on this one landing.
- Rotation automation + key management scripts for the workstation
  age identity.
- Bridge runtime that would turn the implicit admission into explicit
  MaterializationSession records (separate bridge-runtime epic).

Spec adherence:
- SECRET-0002 §2-§4 (BackendProfile / MaterializerProfile / BackendBinding
  structure).
- Must cite wdog-v0-age-local in §1 Purpose and contrast the root-of-trust
  difference (host age key vs. workstation age key).
- Must satisfy the profile-matrix entry (policy/profile-matrix.yaml
  update if applicable; check before editing whether that file is
  profile-indexed).

Acceptance:
- scripts/bridge-conformance-check.sh passes (the existing validator
  for normalized bridge-owned surface).
- New PROFILE.md + README.md + index entry exist under
  specs/secrets/profiles/wdog-operator-workstation-age-local/.
- No changes outside specs/secrets/profiles/ and (if needed) the
  profile-matrix.yaml policy file.
- One visible git commit on a topic branch bridge-?-workstation-
  bootstrap-profile in .git-worktrees/.

Landing:
Default handoff at one visible commit on the topic branch. Branch merge
into main is operator-owned; no push this lane. Close issue only after
merge to main.

Why now:
Workingdoge-o6k ported the Hetzner tofu root; the operator is ready to
mint HCLOUD_TOKEN and run tofu apply, but no admissible profile
currently covers workstation-materialized bootstrap tokens. Without
this profile, the first funded-runtime apply proceeds outside the
SECRET-0002 admission boundary. Filing the profile makes the
admissibility argument complete BEFORE the first apply crosses the
admit line, matching spine §2.5 (secrets decrypt at effect-runtime, in
a declared materialization session).

Dependencies:
- Depends on SECRET-0002 v-current (already normalized).
- Downstream: workingdoge-?? (to be filed) instantiates the profile.
- Downstream: operator rotates any previously-exposed bootstrap tokens
  before the first apply.
close_reason: Merged to bridge main in 1557f72 after bridge-conformance-check and bridge-property-check passed

Issue stays open — landing is merge to bridge main (operator-owned). Downstream workingdoge instantiation is a separate simplex endpoint, to be filed once this lane merges.

title: TRACKER: migrate bridge from embedded Dolt to server mode so tuskd hooks pass
description:
Context:
- The bridge repo tracker is currently in embedded-Dolt mode.
- tuskd and the repo hooks assume server mode, so hook-backed commit flows reject otherwise-valid work.
- This is the bridge-local downstream repair lane corresponding to tusk-154.

Goal:
Migrate the bridge tracker from embedded Dolt to server mode without losing local issue history, then verify the normal hook path works again.

Scope:
- back up the current .beads state before migration
- reinitialize the tracker in server mode
- preserve or restore bridge issue data and local history as needed
- verify bd reports server mode and a trivial commit path no longer fails on the tracker substrate

Non-goals:
- changing tuskd hook policy
- moving bridge workflow ownership back into tusk
- unrelated spec or flake edits except what the migration strictly requires

Verification:
- bd dolt status reports server mode
- a no-op or trivial commit path passes the bridge hooks
- bridge issue data remains present after migration

Landing boundary:
- one visible git commit in a dedicated bridge worktree, plus the migrated local tracker state
notes: 2026-04-15: migrated the canonical bridge tracker from embedded Dolt to server mode. Canonical bd dolt status now reports a running server on 127.0.0.1:49679 with data dir .beads/dolt, and canonical bd doctor passed with warnings only for unrelated dirty git state. Exported 8 issues to /tmp/bridge-ish-issues-pre-server-20260415T040520Z.jsonl and backed up canonical .beads to /tmp/bridge-ish-canonical-beads-pre-server-20260415T040542Z before reinit. Verified a hook-backed git commit in the isolated worktree at commit 5832d2e (bridge-ish: switch bridge tracker metadata to server mode). Also repaired the worktree-local tracker runtime so bd list and bd dolt status work there. bd dolt push completed. Git push of branch bridge-ish-tracker-server-mode is still pending: the first attempt failed on sandbox DNS, the second failed on an unhealthy worktree service record before runtime repair, and the latest SSH batch-mode push is hanging rather than returning a clean success/failure signal.
close_reason: completed on main in commit 1e52ba9; bridge tracker metadata now uses Dolt server mode, issue data is present, and bd status/ready/show plus bd dolt status pass from the bridge dev shell

title: SIGNING: extend the signer-session contract for composite open-session artifacts
description:
Goal:
Extend the bridge-owned signer/session contract so a session-class opener can return the real Tempo open-session artifact family instead of a single signature or opaque tx ref.

Scope:
- define the composite artifact needed for session open: signed transaction bytes, deterministic channelId, cumulativeAmount=0, and initial voucher signature
- keep the work at the contract/type surface first
- line it up with SECRET-0002 realization and the existing signer-session contract
- use the sharpened downstream proof from home-6e7 as the motivating case

Non-goals:
- no downstream home-specific runtime wiring
- no remote publication
- no generic wallet UX work
notes:
Lane launched in .git-worktrees/bridge-5ni-open-session-artifact from main for contract-only work over the composite open-session artifact boundary.
Visible commit 73255c6 adds the typed SessionOpenArtifact surface for composite open-session settlement material over SECRET-0002 realization, wires it into TYPE-FAMILIES and BRIDGE-FLOW, and refreshes the secret-0002 checksum manifest. Verification passed: scripts/bridge-conformance-check.sh; scripts/bridge-property-check.sh. Local main is now at 73255c6.

description:
Goal:
Refresh the bridge identity-admission profile so it explicitly adopts Premath raw/IDENTITY-ADMISSION as the reusable kernel owner rather than only referring to a generic upstream admission core.

Scope:

title: Profile identity-admission semantics onto bridge Authority, Admission, and Realization families
description:
## Goal
Add a bridge-owned profile note that maps the upstream local identity/admission semantic core onto the existing bridge type families and handoff objects.

## Scope
- map principal presentation, assertion, admission, entitlements, and obstructions onto bridge-owned surfaces
- cover the path `AuthorizeRequest -> ProviderResults -> PolicyInput -> Decision -> MaterializationPlanRequest -> MaterializationSession`
- keep the work as a bridge profile note first
- add an index/reference link from the active bridge spec surface

## Non-goals
- do not move the general identity semantic core out of Fish
- do not redefine Premath kernel doctrine inside bridge
- do not start with schema churn unless the profile note exposes a concrete gap
- do not widen into downstream runtime or provider implementation work

## Verification
- the note cleanly maps identity-admission terms onto existing bridge type families
- the note preserves the boundary between upstream kernel law, bridge-domain profile meaning, and downstream runtime proof
- scripts/bridge-conformance-check.sh passes after the note and index link land

## Landing boundary
Stop when bridge has one new profile note plus one stable reference link in the active spec surface and both are committed in one visible change.
close_reason: Completed in visible commit c8cc04c8c68e122952abe44d51255615dbca6357 on branch bridge-z3i-identity-admission-profile

title: BOUNDARY: map NERVE-KB x Kurma x Bridge as the first proof seam
description:
Goal:
Define the first bridge-side boundary and proof seam for consuming the upstream NERVE-KB stack through the carried `kurma` surface without turning `bridge` into the semantic home of NERVE-KB or into a second generic KB runtime.

Context:
- `fish/sites/nerve` already carries the comprehensive NERVE-KB specification pack, including the semantic substrate, vector/routing doctrine, query-planner semantics, answer/session memory, compaction, sync, trust, and proof layers.
- `kurma` already positions itself as the `Premath -> KCIR` carrier/runtime shell, with `Nerve` landing into that substrate rather than bypassing it.
- `bridge` is the likely first real domain-proof consumer for parts of that seam, because bridge already owns canonical witness/admission/materialization boundaries and bridge-specific Premath realization profiles.
- The current risk is repeated boundary drift: `knowledge`, `backend`, and `runtime` language makes `tusk` or `bridge` look like the owner of work that actually belongs in `nerve` semantics or `kurma` carriage.

Topology:
- context class: downstream-proof over a carried seam
- simplex: 2-simplex
- common contract: NERVE-KB witness-carrying semantic substrate as carried through `Premath -> KCIR` into a bridge-domain proof surface
- vertex roles:
  - `nerve`: canonical KB semantics and law
  - `kurma`: carried runtime/compiler realization over `Premath -> KCIR`
  - `bridge`: first bridge-domain proof or consumer seam

Scope:
- define what part of the NERVE-KB / carried seam bridge may legitimately consume as a bridge-domain proof surface
- define what remains upstream semantic substrate in `nerve`
- define what remains carried runtime/compiler work in `kurma`
- define what bridge must not re-own locally when shaping a bridge-domain proof or profile note
- identify the smallest bridge-owned artifact that proves the seam honestly: likely one bridge-domain boundary or profile note, not a runtime implementation
- name the follow-up split for any later `kurma` carriage work and later `tusk` projection work

Non-goals:
- do not implement a generic KB runtime in `bridge`
- do not redefine NERVE-KB semantics inside bridge-owned specs
- do not widen into `tusk` projection or operator-control work in this issue
- do not widen into downstream provider bindings, local secret files, or funded runtime proof
- do not assume bridge should consume the entire KB stack just because it is the first proof consumer

Acceptance:
- bridge has one explicit issue-backed map of the `nerve` / `kurma` / `bridge` proof seam
- the map includes an owner split for semantic substrate, carriage/runtime, and bridge-domain proof
- the issue names the smallest bridge-owned artifact that should be produced in the implementation lane
- the issue states what bridge must not re-own locally
- later work is split by repo boundary rather than mixed into one lane

Verification:
- `bd show <new-issue-id> --json`
- the issue text names the 2-simplex, the common contract, and the three vertex roles explicitly
- the issue text distinguishes semantic substrate from carriage/runtime from bridge-domain proof
- the issue text keeps downstream funded runtime and local provider bindings out of scope

Landing:
- this shaping lane stops at one tracked bridge issue with a clear bridge-owned proof wire; implementation, if needed, happens in a later dedicated bridge lane.

Why now:
- the overall shape is starting to cohere across `nerve`, `kurma`, `bridge`, and `tusk`, but the first real proof consumer needs to be named explicitly before later consumer projection work starts drifting into the wrong repo.
close_reason: landed in visible commit 2ecc004 on main

Remove internal workflow (bd commands) and contributor tools (codex/beads) — those stay in AGENTS.md. See plan at /Users/arj/.claude/plans/replicated-seeking-matsumoto.md

title: PUBLISH: attach a bridge git remote for flake consumers
description:
Goal:
Attach a canonical Git publish remote for bridge so downstream flake consumers can lock a non-machine-local bridge input.

Scope:
- choose and configure the canonical publish remote for this repo
- document the preferred flake ref form for downstream consumers
- verify that a downstream repo can reference bridge by a published Git-backed flake input instead of a local path or git+file dogfood ref

Non-goals:
- do not change the bridge skill content again here
- do not change downstream consumer wiring in this issue
- do not widen into bridge runtime or deployment work

Acceptance:
- bridge has a configured publish remote
- the documented consumer ref points at that published remote with ?ref=main
- a downstream consumer can lock bridge without a machine-local source path
notes: Configured canonical publish remote origin=git@github.com:workingdoge/bridge.git, created public repo https://github.com/workingdoge/bridge, pushed main, and verified both flake and flake=false consumers can lock git+https://github.com/workingdoge/bridge.git?ref=main without a machine-local path.
close_reason: completed with public remote origin=git@github.com:workingdoge/bridge.git, pushed main, and visible doc commit e19fe52 in bridge-2kk-publish-remote

title: SKILL-DEV: export a portable bridge skill surface for flake consumers
description:
Goal:
Make the canonical bridge skill consumable by other repos without machine-local absolute paths. Bridge owns the exported surface; consumers lock a published bridge flake ref instead of pointing at a local checkout.

Scope:
- expose one bridge-owned flake surface for the canonical bridge skill bundle
- document the consumer contract for that exported surface
- name and provide a non-machine-local flake reference path that consumers can lock
- keep bridge as the canonical authoring home for the shared bridge skill

Non-goals:
- do not rewrite bridge-domain doctrine or widen the skill beyond its current canonical content
- do not move bridge-domain ownership into tusk or any consumer repo
- do not implement materialization runtime, providers, or funded deployment proof here

Acceptance:
- bridge exports one consumer-facing flake surface for the canonical bridge skill bundle
- the export contract is documented in-repo
- a non-machine-local flake ref is named or made available for downstream consumers
- a downstream consumer can stop relying on an absolute /Users/... path once this lands

Verification:
- nix flake metadata path:. --json
- rg -n 'bridge-skill|skill bundle|SKILL.md' flake.nix README.md .agents/skills/bridge
- one downstream consumer issue can point at the published contract without a /Users/... source path

Landing Boundary:
- stop at one visible bridge commit plus a named export contract for consumers

title: BRIDGE: define the observation-interpretation family between Authority and Admission
description:
Goal:
Define the canonical bridge-domain interpretation family that assigns stable meaning to foreign observations after authoritative fact resolution and before admission assembly.

Scope:
- add one bridge-domain note that names the observation-interpretation boundary as a first-class family between Authority and Admission
- define the high-level flow from foreign observation to source identity or versioning to interpretation binding to typed observation to admission
- make explicit that decoder registries, caches, and parser selection are subordinate implementation mechanisms, not the semantic center
- explain stronger-than-address source identity and validity-window requirements for interpretation binding
- keep the note general across contract logs, provider payloads, versioned webhooks, and other foreign observation families
- update the bridge type-family map to include Interpretation

Non-goals:
- do not implement runtime storage or cache behavior in this issue
- do not widen into Kurma carrier/runtime realization here
- do not add executable scripting or dynamic decoder semantics
- do not revise secret realization or planner surfaces in this issue

Simplex:
- kind: 1-simplex
- common contract: foreign observation interpretation binding
- vertex A: bridge as canonical meaning owner
- vertex B: kurma as runtime/carrier consumer

Verification:
- bd show <issue-id> --json
- test -f specs/bridge-adapter/interpretation-binding.md
- scripts/bridge-conformance-check.sh

Landing Boundary:
- stop at one tracked Bridge issue, one interpretation-binding note, and one type-family-map update that make the new family explicit
acceptance_criteria:
- Bridge has a tracked issue for the observation-interpretation family
- the spec surface names Interpretation as a family between Authority and Admission
- the landed note includes a high-level topology for foreign observation -> interpretation binding -> typed observation -> admission
- the note treats decoder registry and cache as subordinate runtime mechanisms rather than the semantic center
- the note states stronger-than-address identity and validity-window requirements for admissible interpretation binding
notes: Landed specs/bridge-adapter/interpretation-binding.md as the canonical bridge-domain note for foreign observation interpretation between Authority and Admission. Updated specs/TYPE-FAMILIES.md to insert Interpretation as a first-class family and updated specs/bridge-adapter/README.md so the adapter surface points at the new family. Refreshed specs/bridge-adapter/SHA256SUMS.txt for the added note and README change, then verified with nix run .#bridge-conformance-check.
